texto: Our generation, unfortunately, is stuck to our phones - and, like, Twitter - constantly, which I have no problem with. I'd say we're not describing the children of America or anything like that, but there is something to take from it: It is kind of sad how we can't go thirty minutes without checking our phone.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Israel Broussard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Our generation, unfortunately, is stuck to our phones - and, like, Twitter - constantly, which I …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, children, america pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de america e problem.

texto: I wanted to be a musician, either a guitarist or a drummer. I guess my dreams were in the entertainment industry, and I landed somewhere along there.
